As a signatory to the [Ramsar Convention](/wiki/Ramsar_Convention "Ramsar Convention"), Albania has granted special recognition upon four wetlands, designating them as Wetlands of International Importance, including [Buna](/wiki/Buna_(Adriatic_Sea) "Buna (Adriatic Sea)")-[Shkodër](/wiki/Lake_Skadar "Lake Skadar"), [Butrint](/wiki/Butrint_Lagoon "Butrint Lagoon"), [Karavasta](/wiki/Karavasta_Lagoon "Karavasta Lagoon") and [Prespa](/wiki/Lake_Prespa "Lake Prespa").[[163]](#cite_note-166) The country's dedication to protection extends further into the sphere of [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO")'s [World Network of Biosphere Reserves](/wiki/World_Network_of_Biosphere_Reserves "World Network of Biosphere Reserves"), operating within the framework of the [Man and the Biosphere Programme](/wiki/Man_and_the_Biosphere_Programme "Man and the Biosphere Programme"), evidenced by its engagement in the [Ohrid-Prespa Transboundary Biosphere Reserve](/wiki/Ohrid-Prespa_Transboundary_Biosphere_Reserve "Ohrid-Prespa Transboundary Biosphere Reserve").[[164]](#cite_note-167)[[165]](#cite_note-168) Furthermore, Albania is host to two natural [World Heritage Sites](/wiki/World_Heritage_Site "World Heritage Site"), which encompass the Ohrid region and both the [Gashi River](/wiki/Gashi_River "Gashi River") and [Rrajca](/wiki/Rrajce "Rrajce") as part of [Ancient and Primeval Beech Forests of the Carpathians and Other Regions of Europe](/wiki/Ancient_and_Primeval_Beech_Forests_of_the_Carpathians_and_Other_Regions_of_Europe "Ancient and Primeval Beech Forests of the Carpathians and Other Regions of Europe").[[166]](#cite_note-Ancient_Primeval_Beech_Forests-169)

#### Protected areas

Main article: [Protected areas of Albania](/wiki/Protected_areas_of_Albania "Protected areas of Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/A_fishermen_house_in_Karavasta_Lagoon_%28Divjak%C3%AB-Karavasta_National_Park%29.jpg/250px-A_fishermen_house_in_Karavasta_Lagoon_%28Divjak%C3%AB-Karavasta_National_Park%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:A_fishermen_house_in_Karavasta_Lagoon_(Divjak%C3%AB-Karavasta_National_Park).jpg)

The [lagoon of Karavasta](/wiki/Lagoon_of_Karavasta "Lagoon of Karavasta") within the [Divjakë-Karavasta National Park](/wiki/Divjak%C3%AB-Karavasta_National_Park "Divjakë-Karavasta National Park")

The [protected areas of Albania](/wiki/Protected_areas_of_Albania "Protected areas of Albania") are areas designated and managed by the [Albanian government](/wiki/Albanian_government "Albanian government"). There are 12 [national parks](/wiki/National_park "National park"), 4 [ramsar sites](/wiki/Ramsar_site "Ramsar site"), 1 [biosphere reserve](/wiki/Biosphere_reserve "Biosphere reserve") and 786 other types of conservation reserves in Albania.[[162]](#cite_note-New_NP-165)[[167]](#cite_note-170) Located in the north, the Albanian Alps National Park, comprising the former [Theth National Park](/wiki/Theth_National_Park "Theth National Park") and [Valbonë Valley National Park](/wiki/Valbon%C3%AB_Valley_National_Park "Valbonë Valley National Park"), is surrounded amidst the towering peaks of the [Albanian Alps](/wiki/Accursed_Mountains "Accursed Mountains"). In the east, portions of the rugged [Korab](/wiki/Korab "Korab"), [Nemërçka](/wiki/Nem%C3%ABr%C3%A7ka "Nemërçka") and [Shebenik](/wiki/Shebenik "Shebenik") Mountains are conserved within the boundaries of [Fir of Hotovë-Dangëlli National Park](/wiki/Fir_of_Hotov%C3%AB-Dang%C3%ABlli_National_Park "Fir of Hotovë-Dangëlli National Park"), [Shebenik National Park](/wiki/Shebenik_National_Park "Shebenik National Park") and [Prespa National Park](/wiki/Prespa_National_Park_(Albania) "Prespa National Park (Albania)"), with the latter encompassing Albania's share of the [Great](/wiki/Prespa_Lake "Prespa Lake") and [Small Lakes of Prespa](/wiki/Small_Prespa_Lake "Small Prespa Lake").

To the south, the [Ceraunian Mountains](/wiki/Ceraunian_Mountains "Ceraunian Mountains") define the [Albanian Ionian Sea Coast](/wiki/Albanian_Ionian_Sea_Coast "Albanian Ionian Sea Coast"), shaping the landscape of [Llogara National Park](/wiki/Llogara_National_Park "Llogara National Park"), which extends into the [Karaburun Peninsula](/wiki/Karaburun_Peninsula,_Albania "Karaburun Peninsula, Albania"), forming the [Karaburun-Sazan Marine Park](/wiki/Karaburun-Sazan_Marine_Park "Karaburun-Sazan Marine Park"). Further southward lies [Butrint National Park](/wiki/Butrint_National_Park "Butrint National Park"), occupying a peninsula surrounded by the Lake of Butrint and the [Channel of Vivari](/wiki/Channel_of_Vivari "Channel of Vivari"). In the west, stretching along the [Albanian Adriatic Sea Coast](/wiki/Albanian_Adriatic_Sea_Coast "Albanian Adriatic Sea Coast"), the [Divjakë-Karavasta National Park](/wiki/Divjak%C3%AB-Karavasta_National_Park "Divjakë-Karavasta National Park") boasts the extensive [Lagoon of Karavasta](/wiki/Lagoon_of_Karavasta "Lagoon of Karavasta"), one of the largest lagoon systems in the Mediterranean Sea. Notably, Europe's first wild river national park, [Vjosa National Park](/wiki/Vjosa_Wild_River_National_Park "Vjosa Wild River National Park"), safeguards the [Vjosa River](/wiki/Vjosa "Vjosa") and its primary tributaries, which originates in the [Pindus](/wiki/Pindus "Pindus") Mountains and flows to the Adriatic Sea. [Dajti Mountain National Park](/wiki/Dajti_Mountain_National_Park "Dajti Mountain National Park"), [Lurë-Dejë Mountain National Park](/wiki/Lur%C3%AB-Dej%C3%AB_Mountain_National_Park "Lurë-Dejë Mountain National Park") and [Tomorr Mountain National Park](/wiki/Tomorr_Mountain_National_Park "Tomorr Mountain National Park") protect the mountainous terrain of the centre of Albania, including the [Tomorr](/wiki/Tomorr "Tomorr") and [Skanderbeg Mountains](/wiki/Skanderbeg_Mountains "Skanderbeg Mountains").

#### Environmental issues

Main article: [Environmental issues in Albania](/wiki/Environmental_issues_in_Albania "Environmental issues in Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/Show_Your_Stripes_change_in_temperature_graphic_for_All_of_Albania_with_bars_with_labels.png/250px-Show_Your_Stripes_change_in_temperature_graphic_for_All_of_Albania_with_bars_with_labels.png)](/wiki/File:Show_Your_Stripes_change_in_temperature_graphic_for_All_of_Albania_with_bars_with_labels.png)

Temperature change in Albania. Each bar represents the temperature over a year.

Environmental issues in Albania notably encompass [air](/wiki/Air_pollution "Air pollution") and [water pollution](/wiki/Water_pollution "Water pollution"), [climate change](/wiki/Climate_change_in_Albania "Climate change in Albania") impacts, [waste management](/wiki/Waste_management "Waste management") shortcomings, [biodiversity loss](/wiki/Conservation_biology "Conservation biology") and imperative for [nature conservation](/wiki/Nature_conservation "Nature conservation").[[168]](#cite_note-171)[[169]](#cite_note-172)

Climate change is predicted to exert significant impacts on the quality of life in Albania.[[170]](#cite_note-173) Albania is one of the European countries most at risk and vulnerable to [natural disasters](/wiki/Natural_disaster "Natural disaster").[[171]](#cite_note-174) Natural disasters, such as [floods](/wiki/Flood "Flood"), [forest fires](/wiki/Wildfire "Wildfire"), and [landslides](/wiki/Landslide "Landslide"), are increasing in Albania due to climate change, causing significant damage.[[172]](#cite_note-175)[[173]](#cite_note-176) Rising sea levels are anticipated to negatively impact coastal communities and the tourism industry.[[174]](#cite_note-177)

In 2023 Albania emitted 7.67 million tonnes of [greenhouse gases](/wiki/Greenhouse_gas "Greenhouse gas"),[[175]](#cite_note-178) equivalent to 2.73 tonnes per person,[[176]](#cite_note-179) making it a relatively low emitting country. Albania has pledged a 20.9% reduction in GHG emissions by 2030, and [net zero](/wiki/Net-zero_emissions "Net-zero emissions") by 2050.[[177]](#cite_note-180)

The country has a moderate and improving performance in the [Environmental Performance Index](/wiki/Environmental_Performance_Index "Environmental Performance Index") with an overall ranking of 62 out of 180 countries in 2022.[[178]](#cite_note-181) However, Albania's ranking has decreased since its highest placement at position 15 in the Environmental Performance Index of 2012.[[179]](#cite_note-182)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/02/Prespa_and_Prespa_National_Park_Albania_2017.jpg/1280px-Prespa_and_Prespa_National_Park_Albania_2017.jpg)](/wiki/File:Prespa_and_Prespa_National_Park_Albania_2017.jpg "The Prespa National Park in southeastern Albania is part of the European Green Belt and Ohrid-Prespa Biosphere Reserve.")

The [Prespa National Park](/wiki/Prespa_National_Park_(Albania) "Prespa National Park (Albania)") in southeastern Albania is part of the [European Green Belt](/wiki/European_Green_Belt "European Green Belt") and [Ohrid-Prespa Biosphere Reserve](/wiki/Ohrid-Prespa_Transboundary_Biosphere_Reserve "Ohrid-Prespa Transboundary Biosphere Reserve").

## Politics

Main article: [Politics of Albania](/wiki/Politics_of_Albania "Politics of Albania")

See also: [Government of Albania](/wiki/Government_of_Albania "Government of Albania")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |
| [Bajram Begaj](/wiki/Bajram_Begaj "Bajram Begaj") [President](/wiki/President_of_Albania "President of Albania") | [Edi Rama](/wiki/Edi_Rama "Edi Rama") [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Albania "Prime Minister of Albania") |

Since declaring independence in 1912, Albania has experienced a significant political transformation, traversing through distinct periods that included a monarchical rule, a communist regime and the eventual establishment of a democratic order.[[180]](#cite_note-183) In 1998, Albania transitioned into a [sovereign](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state") [parliamentary](/wiki/Parliamentary_republic "Parliamentary republic") [constitutional republic](/wiki/Constitutional_republic "Constitutional republic"), marking a fundamental milestone in its political evolution.[[181]](#cite_note-Kushtetuta-184) Its governance structure operates under a [constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_Albania "Constitution of Albania") that serves as the principal document of the country.[[182]](#cite_note-185) The constitution is grounded in the principle of the [separation of powers](/wiki/Separation_of_powers "Separation of powers"), with three arms of government that encompass the legislative embodied in the [Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Albania "Parliament of Albania"), the executive led by the [President](/wiki/President_of_Albania "President of Albania") as the ceremonial [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") and the [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Albania "Prime Minister of Albania") as the functional [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government"), and the judiciary with a hierarchy of courts, including the [constitutional](/wiki/Constitutional_Court_of_Albania "Constitutional Court of Albania") and [supreme courts](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_Albania "Supreme Court of Albania") as well as multiple [appeal](/wiki/Appeals_courts_of_Albania "Appeals courts of Albania") and [administrative courts](/wiki/Administrative_Courts_in_Albania "Administrative Courts in Albania").[[181]](#cite_note-Kushtetuta-184)

Albania's legal system is structured to protect its people's political rights, regardless of their ethnic, linguistic, racial, or religious affiliations.[[181]](#cite_note-Kushtetuta-184)[[183]](#cite_note-FH2023-186) Despite these principles, there are significant human rights concerns in Albania that demand attention.[[184]](#cite_note-USDS_Politics-187) These concerns include issues related to the independence of the judiciary, the absence of a free media sector and the enduring problem of corruption within various governmental bodies, law enforcement agencies and other institutions.[[184]](#cite_note-USDS_Politics-187) As Albania pursues its path towards EU membership, active efforts are being made to achieve substantial improvements in these areas to align with EU criteria and standards.[[183]](#cite_note-FH2023-186)

### Foreign relations

Main article: [Foreign relations of Albania](/wiki/Foreign_relations_of_Albania "Foreign relations of Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/36/Artigianato_Arberesh.jpg/250px-Artigianato_Arberesh.jpg)](/wiki/File:Artigianato_Arberesh.jpg)

Assisted by the governments of [Kosovo](/wiki/Kosovo "Kosovo") and Albania, an official application for the inclusion of the [Arbëreshë people](/wiki/Arb%C3%ABresh%C3%AB_people "Arbëreshë people") in the list of [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO")'s [Intangible Cultural Heritage](/wiki/UNESCO_Intangible_Cultural_Heritage_Lists "UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage Lists") is being prepared.[[185]](#cite_note-188)

Emerging from decades of isolation following the fall of communism, Albania has adopted a [foreign policy](/wiki/Foreign_relations_of_Albania "Foreign relations of Albania") orientation centred on active cooperation and engagement in international affairs. At the core of Albania's foreign policies lie a set of objectives, which encompass the commitment to protect its sovereignty and territorial integrity, the cultivation of diplomatic ties with other countries, advocating for [international recognition of Kosovo](/wiki/International_recognition_of_Kosovo "International recognition of Kosovo"), addressing the concerns related to the [expulsion of Cham Albanians](/wiki/Expulsion_of_Cham_Albanians "Expulsion of Cham Albanians"), pursuing Euro-Atlantic integration and protecting the rights of the Albanians in [Kosovo](/wiki/Albanians_in_Kosovo "Albanians in Kosovo"), [Greece](/wiki/Albanians_in_greece "Albanians in greece"), [Italy](/wiki/Arb%C3%ABresh%C3%AB_people "Arbëreshë people"), [Montenegro](/wiki/Albanians_in_Montenegro "Albanians in Montenegro"), [North Macedonia](/wiki/Albanians_in_Macedonia "Albanians in Macedonia"), [Serbia](/wiki/Albanians_in_south_Serbia "Albanians in south Serbia") and the [diaspora](/wiki/Albanian_diaspora "Albanian diaspora").[[186]](#cite_note-189)

The external affairs of Albania underscore the country's dedication to regional stability and integration into major international institutions.[[187]](#cite_note-190) Albania became a member of the [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") (UN) in 1955, shortly after emerging from a period of isolation during the communist era.[[188]](#cite_note-191) The country reached a major achievement in its foreign policy by securing NATO membership in 2009.[[189]](#cite_note-192)[[190]](#cite_note-193) Since obtaining candidate status in 2014, the country has also embarked on a comprehensive reform agenda to align itself with European Union (EU) accession standards, with the objective of becoming an EU member state.[[122]](#cite_note-EU_CS-125)

Albania and Kosovo maintain a fraternal relationship strengthened by their substantial cultural, ethnical and historical ties.[[191]](#cite_note-AlbKosRelation-194) Both countries foster enduring diplomatic ties, with Albania actively supporting Kosovo's development and international integration efforts.[[191]](#cite_note-AlbKosRelation-194) Its fundamental contribution to [Kosovo's path to independence](/wiki/2008_Kosovo_declaration_of_independence "2008 Kosovo declaration of independence") is underscored by its early [recognition of Kosovo's sovereignty](/wiki/International_recognition_of_Kosovo "International recognition of Kosovo") in 2008.[[192]](#cite_note-195) Furthermore, both governments hold annual joint meetings, displayed by the inaugural meeting in 2014, which serves as an official platform to enhance bilateral cooperation and reinforce their joint commitment to policies that promote the stability and prosperity of the broader Albanian region.[[191]](#cite_note-AlbKosRelation-194)

### Military

Main article: [Armed Forces of Albania](/wiki/Armed_Forces_of_Albania "Armed Forces of Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1f/Albanian_special_operations_forces%2C_provide_security_as_Afghan_Border_Police_%28ABP%29_break_ground_on_a_new_checkpoint_in_the_district_of_Spin_Boldak%2C_Kandahar_province%2C_Afghanistan%2C_March_25%2C_2013_130325-A-MX357-127.jpg/250px-thumbnail.jpg)](/wiki/File:Albanian_special_operations_forces,_provide_security_as_Afghan_Border_Police_(ABP)_break_ground_on_a_new_checkpoint_in_the_district_of_Spin_Boldak,_Kandahar_province,_Afghanistan,_March_25,_2013_130325-A-MX357-127.jpg)

[Albanian soldiers](/wiki/Albanian_Land_Force "Albanian Land Force") in the [Province of Kandahar](/wiki/Kandahar_Province "Kandahar Province"), Afghanistan

The [Albanian Armed Forces](/wiki/Albanian_Armed_Forces "Albanian Armed Forces") consist of [Land](/wiki/Albanian_Land_Force "Albanian Land Force"), [Air](/wiki/Albanian_Air_Force "Albanian Air Force") and [Naval Forces](/wiki/Albanian_Naval_Force "Albanian Naval Force") and constitute the military and paramilitary forces of the country. They are led by a [commander-in-chief](/wiki/Commander-in-chief "Commander-in-chief") under the supervision of the [Ministry of Defence](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(Albania) "Ministry of Defence (Albania)") and by the [President](/wiki/President_of_Albania "President of Albania") as the supreme commander during wartime. However, in times of peace its powers are executed through the [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Albania "Prime Minister of Albania") and the [Defence Minister](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(Albania) "Ministry of Defence (Albania)").[[193]](#cite_note-constitution-196)

The chief purpose of the armed forces of Albania is the defence of the independence, the [sovereignty](/wiki/Sovereignty "Sovereignty") and the [territorial integrity](/wiki/Territorial_integrity "Territorial integrity") of the country, as well as the participation in humanitarian, combat, non-combat and peace support operations.[[193]](#cite_note-constitution-196) [Military service](/wiki/Military_service "Military service") is voluntary since 2010 with the age of 19 being the legal minimum age for the duty.[[194]](#cite_note-197)[[195]](#cite_note-198)

Albania has committed to increase the participations in multinational operations.[[196]](#cite_note-199) Since the fall of communism, the country has participated in six international missions but only one United Nations mission in [Georgia](/wiki/UNOMIG "UNOMIG"), where it sent three military observers. Since February 2008, Albania has participated officially in NATO's [Operation Active Endeavor](/wiki/Operation_Active_Endeavor "Operation Active Endeavor") in the [Mediterranean Sea](/wiki/Mediterranean_Sea "Mediterranean Sea").[[197]](#cite_note-200) It was invited to join NATO on 3 April 2008, and it became a full member on 2 April 2009.[[198]](#cite_note-201)

Albania reduced the number of active troops from 65,000 in 1988 to 14,500 in 2009.[[199]](#cite_note-202)[[200]](#cite_note-203) The military now consists mainly of a small fleet of aircraft and sea vessels. Increasing the military budget was one of the most important conditions for [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") integration. As of 1996 military spending was an estimated 1.5% of the country's GDP, only to peak in 2009 at 2% and fall again to 1.5%.[[201]](#cite_note-204)

### Human rights

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/Tirana_Gay%28P%29Ride_2016.jpg/250px-Tirana_Gay%28P%29Ride_2016.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tirana_Gay(P)Ride_2016.jpg)

2016 LGBT+ Pride in [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana "Tirana")

Nearly 60% of women in rural areas suffer physical or psychological violence and nearly 8% are victims of sexual violence.
Protection orders are often violated.
In 2014 the Albanian Helsinki Committee (AHC) reported that the number of female murder victims is still high.[[202]](#cite_note-civilRightsDefenders-205) The Commissioner for Protection from Discrimination has raised concerns regarding the family registration law that discriminates against women. As a result, heads of households, who are overwhelmingly men, have the right to change family residency without their partners' permission.[[202]](#cite_note-civilRightsDefenders-205)

In 2015, the association [ILGA-Europe](/wiki/ILGA-Europe "ILGA-Europe") ranked Albania 19th in terms of LGBT rights out of 49 observed European countries.[[203]](#cite_note-206) On the latest report in 2022, lack of progress caused Albania to be ranked the 28th country in Europe, among 49 countries observed.[[204]](#cite_note-207)

The [counties of Albania](/wiki/Counties_of_Albania "Counties of Albania") are the first-level administrative units in Albania.

Albania is defined within a territorial area of 28,748 km2 (11,100 sq mi) in the [Balkan Peninsula](/wiki/Balkan_Peninsula "Balkan Peninsula"). It is informally divided into three regions, the [Northern](/wiki/Northern_Albania "Northern Albania"), [Central](/wiki/Central_Albania "Central Albania") and [Southern Regions](/wiki/Southern_Albania "Southern Albania"). Since [its Declaration of Independence](/wiki/Albanian_Declaration_of_Independence "Albanian Declaration of Independence") in 1912, Albania has reformed [its internal organisation](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Albania#History "Administrative divisions of Albania") 21 times. Presently, the primary [administrative units](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Albania "Administrative divisions of Albania") are the twelve constituent [counties](/wiki/Counties_of_Albania "Counties of Albania") (*[qarqe](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/qarqe#Albanian "wikt:qarqe")/[qarqet](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/qarqet#Albanian "wikt:qarqet")*), which hold equal status under the law.[[205]](#cite_note-208) Counties had previously been used in the 1950s and were recreated on 31 July 2000 to unify the 36 [districts](/wiki/Districts_of_Albania "Districts of Albania") (*[rrathë](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/rrath%C3%AB#Albanian "wikt:rrathë")/[rrathët](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/rrath%C3%ABt#Albanian "wikt:rrathët")*) of that time.[[206]](#cite_note-209)[[207]](#cite_note-terref-210) The largest county in Albania by population is [Tirana County](/wiki/Tirana_County "Tirana County") with over 800,000 people. The smallest county, by population, is [Gjirokastër County](/wiki/Gjirokast%C3%ABr_County "Gjirokastër County") with over 70,000 people. The largest county, by area, is [Korçë County](/wiki/Kor%C3%A7%C3%AB_County "Korçë County") encompassing 3,711 square kilometres (1,433 sq mi) of the southeast of Albania. The smallest county, by area, is [Durrës County](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs_County "Durrës County") with an area of 766 square kilometres (296 sq mi) in the west of Albania.

The counties are made up of 61 second-level divisions known as [municipalities](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Albania "Municipalities of Albania") (*[bashki](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/bashki#Albanian "wikt:bashki")/[bashkia](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/bashkia#Albanian "wikt:bashkia")*).[[208]](#cite_note-211) The municipalities are the first level of local governance, responsible for local needs and [law enforcement](/wiki/Law_enforcement_in_Albania "Law enforcement in Albania").[[209]](#cite_note-212)[[210]](#cite_note-213)[[211]](#cite_note-214) They unified and simplified the previous system of [urban](/wiki/Urban_municipalities_of_Albania "Urban municipalities of Albania") and [rural municipalities](/wiki/Rural_municipalities_of_Albania "Rural municipalities of Albania") or [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_Albania "Communes of Albania") (*[komuna](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/komuna#Albanian "wikt:komuna")/[komunat](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/komunat#Albanian "wikt:komunat")*) in 2015.[[212]](#cite_note-215)[[213]](#cite_note-216) For smaller issues of [local government](/wiki/Local_government "Local government"), the municipalities are organised into 373 [administrative units](/wiki/Administrative_units_of_Albania "Administrative units of Albania") (*njësia*/*njësitë administrative*). There are also 2980 villages (*[fshatra](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/fshatra#Albanian "wikt:fshatra")/[fshatrat](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/fshatrat#Albanian "wikt:fshatrat")*), neighborhoods or wards (*[lagje](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/lagje#Albanian "wikt:lagje")/[lagjet](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/lagjet#Albanian "wikt:lagjet")*), and localities (*[lokalitete](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/lokalitete#Albanian "wikt:lokalitete")/[lokalitetet](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/lokalitetet#Albanian "wikt:lokalitetet")*) previously used as administrative units.

## Economy

Main article: [Economy of Albania](/wiki/Economy_of_Albania "Economy of Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/35/Toptani_Shopping_Mall_Tirana_2016.jpg/250px-Toptani_Shopping_Mall_Tirana_2016.jpg)](/wiki/File:Toptani_Shopping_Mall_Tirana_2016.jpg)

The metropolitan area of Durana, which encompasses the cities of [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana "Tirana") and [Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs "Durrës"), functions as the principal economic hub of Albania.[[216]](#cite_note-219)

Transitioning from a [socialist](/wiki/Socialism "Socialism") [planned](/wiki/Planned_economy "Planned economy") to a [capitalist](/wiki/Capitalism "Capitalism") [mixed economy](/wiki/Mixed_economy "Mixed economy"), Albania is classified as a [developing](/wiki/Developing_country "Developing country") [open market](/wiki/Open_market "Open market") with an [upper-middle income](/wiki/List_of_countries_by_GNI_(nominal,_Atlas_method)_per_capita#Upper-middle-income_group "List of countries by GNI (nominal, Atlas method) per capita") status.[[217]](#cite_note-220)[[218]](#cite_note-221) The [Albanian economy](/wiki/Economy_of_Albania "Economy of Albania") is characterised by a substantial [service sector](/wiki/Tertiary_sector_of_the_economy "Tertiary sector of the economy"), comprising 48.6% of GDP, with key contributions from wholesale and retail trade, [tourism](/wiki/Tourism_in_Albania "Tourism in Albania"), real estate, and logistics.[[219]](#cite_note-Eco:_Sectors-222) Following this, the [industrial sector](/wiki/Secondary_sector_of_the_economy "Secondary sector of the economy") accounts for 20.2%, particularly influenced by construction, while the [agricultural sector](/wiki/Primary_sector_of_the_economy "Primary sector of the economy"), including forestry, and fisheries, represents 18.5%, and the [manufacturing sector](/wiki/Secondary_sector_of_the_economy "Secondary sector of the economy") contributes a modest 6.3% to the economy.[[219]](#cite_note-Eco:_Sectors-222) As of 2025, Albania's nominal [gross domestic product](/wiki/Gross_domestic_product "Gross domestic product") (GDP) is reported at $26.911 billion with a [GDP per capita](/wiki/Gross_domestic_product#Nominal_GDP_and_adjustments_to_GDP "Gross domestic product") of $9,474.[[220]](#cite_note-223) The projected annual percentage change in economic growth for 2025 is 3.8%, with a forecasted moderation to 3.5% by 2029.[[221]](#cite_note-224)

Albania has developed a comprehensive framework for international trade through several pivotal agreements with various regions. The [Stabilisation and Association Agreement](/wiki/Stabilisation_and_Association_Process "Stabilisation and Association Process") (SAA) with the European Union (EU), signed in 2006, is one of the most significant.[[222]](#cite_note-225) This agreement provides critical trade benefits and serves as a foundation for the Albanian integration into the EU. In 2009, Albania signed a free trade agreement (FTA) with the [European Free Trade Association](/wiki/European_Free_Trade_Association "European Free Trade Association") (EFTA), which further expands trade opportunities and market access.[[223]](#cite_note-226) The country is also a signatory to the [Central European Free Trade Agreement](/wiki/Central_European_Free_Trade_Agreement "Central European Free Trade Agreement") (CEFTA) and [Organization of the Black Sea Economic Cooperation](/wiki/Organization_of_the_Black_Sea_Economic_Cooperation "Organization of the Black Sea Economic Cooperation") (BSEC), promoting regional trade and collaboration with neighboring countries.[[224]](#cite_note-227)[[225]](#cite_note-228) Fruthermore, the country has enhanced its bilateral trade relations through a FTA with Turkey, the United Kingdom and a bilateral investment treaty with the United States.[[226]](#cite_note-229)[[227]](#cite_note-230)

The 2023 [Economic Freedom of the World](/wiki/Economic_Freedom_of_the_World "Economic Freedom of the World") report placed Albania in the 31st position, noting a considerable degree of economic independence and the effective adoption of market-oriented policies.[[228]](#cite_note-Economic_Freedom_of_the_World_2023-231) The 2024 [Bertelsmann Transformation Index](/wiki/Bertelsmann_Transformation_Index "Bertelsmann Transformation Index") ranked Albania 20th and acknowledged substantial progress in political and economic transformation.[[229]](#cite_note-Bertelsmann_Transformation_Index_2024-232) Also in 2024, the country was positioned 23rd in the [Global Gender Gap Index](/wiki/Global_Gender_Gap_Report "Global Gender Gap Report"), excelling in the Economic Participation and Opportunity category with a global ranking of 16th, showcasing significant advancements in women's economic participation.[[230]](#cite_note-233) However, the Albanian economy, despite demonstrating considerable resilience, encounters substantial vulnerabilities that hinder its development.[[231]](#cite_note-ICS_US-234) Principal challenges include widespread corruption, distorted competition, and frequent modifications to fiscal legislation.[[231]](#cite_note-ICS_US-234) Furthermore, inadequate contract enforcement, a large informal sector, and persistent labor shortages further complicate the national economic progress.[[229]](#cite_note-Bertelsmann_Transformation_Index_2024-232)[[231]](#cite_note-ICS_US-234)

Economic landscape of Albania is characterised by regional disparities, particularly apparent in the concentration of economic activities in the triangular region formed by [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana "Tirana"), [Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs "Durrës") and [Laç](/wiki/La%C3%A7 "Laç").[[232]](#cite_note-235) This metropolitan area, referred to as Durana, functions as the principal economic center of Albania.[[233]](#cite_note-236)[[234]](#cite_note-237) In the year 2021, [Tirana County](/wiki/Tirana_County "Tirana County") accounted for a significant 44% of the national GDP, with the counties of [Fier](/wiki/Fier_County "Fier County") and [Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs_County "Durrës County") also playing integral roles in the economy, contributing 11% and 10% to the GDP, respectively.[[235]](#cite_note-GDP_by_County-238) Other substantial economic centers, such as [Elbasan](/wiki/Elbasan "Elbasan") and the coastal cities of [Sarandë](/wiki/Sarand%C3%AB "Sarandë") and [Vlorë](/wiki/Vlor%C3%AB "Vlorë"), highlight the uneven distribution of economic development across the country.[[236]](#cite_note-239) Cultural regions, including [Berat](/wiki/Berat "Berat") and [Korçë](/wiki/Kor%C3%A7%C3%AB "Korçë"), despite their geographical distance from the primary economic hubs, have derived considerable benefits from the expansion of the tourism sector.[[237]](#cite_note-240)

### Primary sector

Main article: [Agriculture in Albania](/wiki/Agriculture_in_Albania "Agriculture in Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/15/Berat_kale_-_panoramio.jpg/250px-Berat_kale_-_panoramio.jpg)](/wiki/File:Berat_kale_-_panoramio.jpg)

Grapes in [Berat](/wiki/Berat "Berat"). Due to the [Mediterranean climate](/wiki/Mediterranean_climate "Mediterranean climate"), [wine](/wiki/Albanian_wine "Albanian wine"), [olives](/wiki/Olive "Olive") and [citrus fruits](/wiki/Citrus_fruit "Citrus fruit") are mostly produced in Southern Albania.

Albania produces significant amounts of fruits (apples, [olives](/wiki/Olive "Olive"), grapes, oranges, lemons, [apricots](/wiki/Apricot "Apricot"), [peaches](/wiki/Peach "Peach"), [cherries](/wiki/Cherry "Cherry"), [figs](/wiki/Figs "Figs"), [sour cherries](/wiki/Prunus_cerasus "Prunus cerasus"), [plums](/wiki/Plum "Plum"), and [strawberries](/wiki/Strawberry "Strawberry")), [vegetables](/wiki/Vegetable_oil "Vegetable oil") (potatoes, tomatoes, [maize](/wiki/Maize "Maize"), onions, and wheat), [sugar beets](/wiki/Sugar_beet "Sugar beet"), tobacco, meat, [honey](/wiki/Honey "Honey"), [dairy products](/wiki/Dairy_product "Dairy product"), traditional medicine and [aromatic plants](/wiki/Essential_oil "Essential oil"). Further, the country is a worldwide significant producer of [salvia](/wiki/Salvia "Salvia"), [rosemary](/wiki/Rosemary "Rosemary") and [yellow gentian](/wiki/Gentiana_lutea "Gentiana lutea").[[238]](#cite_note-241) The country's proximity to the [Ionian Sea](/wiki/Ionian_Sea "Ionian Sea") and the [Adriatic Sea](/wiki/Adriatic_Sea "Adriatic Sea") give the underdeveloped fishing industry great potential. The [World Bank](/wiki/World_Bank "World Bank") and [European Community](/wiki/European_Community "European Community") economists report that, Albania's fishing industry has good potential to generate export earnings because prices in the nearby Greek and Italian markets are many times higher than those in the Albanian market. The fish available off the coasts of the country are [carp](/wiki/Carp "Carp"), [trout](/wiki/Trout "Trout"), [sea bream](/wiki/Sea_bream "Sea bream"), [mussels](/wiki/Mussel "Mussel") and [crustaceans](/wiki/Crustacean "Crustacean").

Albania has one of Europe's longest histories of [viticulture](/wiki/Viticulture "Viticulture").[[239]](#cite_note-winealbania1-242) Today's region was one of the few places where vine was naturally grown during the ice age. The oldest found seeds in the region are 4,000 to 6,000 years old.[[240]](#cite_note-Stevenson2011-243) In 2009, the nation produced an estimated 17,500 tonnes of wine.[[241]](#cite_note-244)

### Secondary sector

See also: [Category:Mines in Albania](/wiki/Category:Mines_in_Albania "Category:Mines in Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f9/Cement_factory_in_Fush%C3%AB-Kruj%C3%AB%2C_Albania_%2810759257413%29.jpg/250px-Cement_factory_in_Fush%C3%AB-Kruj%C3%AB%2C_Albania_%2810759257413%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cement_factory_in_Fush%C3%AB-Kruj%C3%AB,_Albania_(10759257413).jpg)

The [Antea](/wiki/Antea_Cement "Antea Cement") factory in [Fushë-Krujë](/wiki/Fush%C3%AB-Kruj%C3%AB "Fushë-Krujë")

Albania's secondary sector has undergone many changes and diversification since the communist regime collapsed. It is very diversified, from [electronics](/wiki/Electronics_industry "Electronics industry"), [manufacturing](/wiki/Manufacturing_industries "Manufacturing industries"),[[242]](#cite_note-245) [textiles](/wiki/Textile_industry "Textile industry"), to [food](/wiki/Food_industry "Food industry"), [cement](/wiki/Cement_industry "Cement industry"), [mining](/wiki/Mining_industry "Mining industry"),[[243]](#cite_note-246) and [energy](/wiki/Energy_industry "Energy industry"). The [Antea Cement](/wiki/Antea_Cement "Antea Cement") plant in [Fushë-Krujë](/wiki/Fush%C3%AB-Kruj%C3%AB "Fushë-Krujë") is considered one of the nation's largest industrial greenfield investments.[[244]](#cite_note-247) Albanian oil and gas is one of the most promising, albeit strictly regulated, sectors of its economy. Albania has the [second-largest oil deposits](/wiki/List_of_countries_by_proven_oil_reserves "List of countries by proven oil reserves") in the [Balkan peninsula](/wiki/Balkan_peninsula "Balkan peninsula") after [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), and the largest [oil reserves](/wiki/Patos-Marinza_Oil_Field "Patos-Marinza Oil Field")[[245]](#cite_note-248) in Europe. The [Albpetrol](/wiki/Albpetrol "Albpetrol") company is owned by the Albanian state and monitors the state petroleum agreements in the country. The textile industry has seen an extensive expansion by approaching companies from the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") (EU) in Albania. According to the [Instituti i Statistikës (INSTAT)](/wiki/Institute_of_Statistics_(Albania) "Institute of Statistics (Albania)"), as of 2016[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Albania&action=edit), textile production had an annual growth of 5.3% and an annual turnover of around 1.5 billion euros.[[246]](#cite_note-249)

Albania is a significant minerals producer and ranks among the world's leading [chromium](/wiki/Chromium "Chromium") producers and exporters.[[247]](#cite_note-250) The nation is also a notable producer of copper, nickel, and coal.[[248]](#cite_note-251) The [Batra mine](/wiki/Batra_mine "Batra mine"), [Bulqizë mine](/wiki/Bulqiz%C3%AB_mine "Bulqizë mine"), and [Thekna mine](/wiki/Thekna_mine "Thekna mine") are among the most recognised Albanian mines still in operation.

### Tertiary sector

See also: [Banking](/wiki/Banking_in_Albania "Banking in Albania"), [Telecommunications](/wiki/Telecommunications_in_Albania "Telecommunications in Albania"), and [Tourism in Albania](/wiki/Tourism_in_Albania "Tourism in Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Ksamil_Albania_._Albanian_Riviera.jpg/250px-Ksamil_Albania_._Albanian_Riviera.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ksamil_Albania_._Albanian_Riviera.jpg)

The [Islets of Ksamil](/wiki/Islets_of_Ksamil "Islets of Ksamil"), in the south of the [Albanian Ionian Sea Coast](/wiki/Albanian_Ionian_Sea_Coast "Albanian Ionian Sea Coast")

The [tertiary sector](/wiki/Tertiary_sector "Tertiary sector") represents the fastest growing sector of the country's economy. 36% of the population work in the service sector which contributes to 65% of the country's GDP.[[249]](#cite_note-DSSA-252) Ever since the end of the 20th century, the [banking industry](/wiki/Banking_industry "Banking industry") is a major component of the tertiary sector and remains in good conditions overall due to [privatisation](/wiki/Privatisation "Privatisation") and the commendable [monetary policy](/wiki/Monetary_policy "Monetary policy").[[250]](#cite_note-253)[[249]](#cite_note-DSSA-252)

Previously one of the most [isolated](/wiki/Isolationism "Isolationism") and controlled countries in the world, [telecommunication industry](/wiki/Telecommunication_industry "Telecommunication industry") represents nowadays another major contributor to the sector. It developed largely through privatisation and subsequent investment by both domestic and foreign investors.[[249]](#cite_note-DSSA-252) [Eagle](/wiki/Eagle_Mobile "Eagle Mobile"), [Vodafone](/wiki/Vodafone_Albania "Vodafone Albania") and [Telekom Albania](/wiki/Telekom_Albania "Telekom Albania") are the leading [telecommunications service providers](/wiki/Telecommunications_service_provider "Telecommunications service provider") in the country.

Tourism is recognised as an industry of national importance and has been steadily increasing since the beginnings of the 21st century.[[251]](#cite_note-254)[[252]](#cite_note-255) It directly accounted for 8.4% of GDP in 2016 though including indirect contributions pushes the proportion to 26%.[[253]](#cite_note-256) In the same year, the country received approximately 4.74 million visitors mostly from across Europe and the United States as well.[[254]](#cite_note-257)

The increase of foreign visitors has been dramatic. Albania had only 500,000 visitors in 2005, and an estimated 4.2 million in 2012, an increase of 740 percent. According to the [United Nations World Tourism Organization](/wiki/UN_Tourism "UN Tourism") (UN Tourism), Albania welcomed approximately 11.7 million tourists in 2024, marking a significant increase from previous years.[[255]](#cite_note-258)

The bulk of the tourist industry is concentrated along the [Adriatic](/wiki/Adriatic "Adriatic") and [Ionian Sea](/wiki/Ionian_Sea "Ionian Sea") in the west of the country. But the [Albanian Riviera](/wiki/Albanian_Riviera "Albanian Riviera") in the southwest has the most scenic and pristine beaches; its coastline has a considerable length of 446 kilometres (277 miles).[[256]](#cite_note-259) The coast has a distinctive character, rich in varieties of virgin beaches, capes, coves, covered bays, lagoons, small gravel beaches, sea caves, and many landforms. Some parts of this seaside are very clean ecologically, including unexplored areas, which are very rare within the [Mediterranean](/wiki/Mediterranean "Mediterranean").[[257]](#cite_note-260) Other attractions include the mountainous areas such as the [Albanian Alps](/wiki/Albanian_Alps "Albanian Alps"), [Ceraunian Mountains](/wiki/Ceraunian_Mountains "Ceraunian Mountains") and [Korab](/wiki/Korab "Korab") Mountains but also the historical cities of [Berat](/wiki/Berat "Berat"), [Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs "Durrës"), [Gjirokastër](/wiki/Gjirokast%C3%ABr "Gjirokastër"), [Sarandë](/wiki/Sarand%C3%AB "Sarandë"), [Shkodër](/wiki/Shkod%C3%ABr "Shkodër") and [Korçë](/wiki/Kor%C3%A7%C3%AB "Korçë").

### Transport

Main article: [Transport in Albania](/wiki/Transport_in_Albania "Transport in Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Autostrada_Durr%C3%ABs-Morina-05.jpg/250px-Autostrada_Durr%C3%ABs-Morina-05.jpg)](/wiki/File:Autostrada_Durr%C3%ABs-Morina-05.jpg)

[Rruga e Kombit](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs-Kuk%C3%ABs_Highway "Durrës-Kukës Highway") connects the [Adriatic Sea](/wiki/Adriatic_Sea "Adriatic Sea") across the [Western Lowlands](/wiki/Western_Lowlands "Western Lowlands") with the [Albanian Alps](/wiki/Albanian_Alps "Albanian Alps").

Transportation in Albania is managed within the functions of the [Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy](/wiki/Ministry_of_Infrastructure_and_Energy_(Albania) "Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy (Albania)") and entities such as the [Albanian Road Authority](/wiki/Albanian_Road_Authority "Albanian Road Authority") (ARRSH), responsible for the construction and maintenance of the [highways](/wiki/Highways_in_Albania "Highways in Albania") and [motorways](/wiki/Motorways_in_Albania "Motorways in Albania") in Albania, as well as the [Albanian Aviation Authority](/wiki/Civil_Aviation_Authority_(Albania) "Civil Aviation Authority (Albania)") (AAC), with the responsibility of coordinating civil aviation and airports in the country.

The [international airport](/wiki/Tirana_International_Airport_N%C3%ABn%C3%AB_Tereza "Tirana International Airport Nënë Tereza") of [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana "Tirana") is the premier air gateway to the country, and is also the principal hub for Albania's national [flag carrier](/wiki/Flag_carrier "Flag carrier") airline, [Air Albania](/wiki/Air_Albania "Air Albania"). The airport carried more than 3.3 million passengers in 2019 with connections to many destinations in other countries around [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe"), [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa") and [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia").[[258]](#cite_note-261) The country plans to progressively increase the number of airports especially in the south with possible locations in [Sarandë](/wiki/Sarand%C3%AB "Sarandë"), [Gjirokastër](/wiki/Gjirokast%C3%ABr "Gjirokastër") and [Vlorë](/wiki/Vlor%C3%AB_International_Airport "Vlorë International Airport").[[259]](#cite_note-262)

The [highways](/wiki/Highways_in_Albania "Highways in Albania") and [motorways in Albania](/wiki/Motorways_in_Albania "Motorways in Albania") are properly maintained and often still under construction and renovation. The [Autostrada 1](/wiki/A1_motorway_(Albania) "A1 motorway (Albania)") (A1) is an integral transportation corridor and the country's longest motorway. It is planned to link [Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs "Durrës") on the Adriatic Sea across [Pristina](/wiki/Pristina "Pristina") in Kosovo with the [Pan-European Corridor X](/wiki/Pan-European_Corridor_X "Pan-European Corridor X") in Serbia.[[260]](#cite_note-SEETO-263)[[261]](#cite_note-264) The [Autostrada 2](/wiki/A2_motorway_(Albania) "A2 motorway (Albania)") (A2) is part of the [Adriatic–Ionian Corridor](/wiki/Adriatic%E2%80%93Ionian_motorway "Adriatic–Ionian motorway") as well as the [Pan-European Corridor VIII](/wiki/Pan-European_Corridor_VIII "Pan-European Corridor VIII") and connects [Fier](/wiki/Fier "Fier") with [Vlorë](/wiki/Vlor%C3%AB "Vlorë").[[260]](#cite_note-SEETO-263) The [Autostrada 3](/wiki/A3_motorway_(Albania) "A3 motorway (Albania)") (A3) is under construction and after its completion will connect [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana "Tirana") and [Elbasan](/wiki/Elbasan "Elbasan") with the Pan-European Corridor VIII. When all three corridors are completed, Albania will have an estimated 759 kilometres (472 mi) of highway, linking it with all neighboring countries.

[Durrës](/wiki/Port_of_Durr%C3%ABs "Port of Durrës") is the busiest and largest [seaport](/wiki/Seaport "Seaport") in the country, followed by [Vlorë](/wiki/Port_of_Vlor%C3%AB "Port of Vlorë"), [Shëngjin](/wiki/Port_of_Sh%C3%ABngjin "Port of Shëngjin") and [Sarandë](/wiki/Port_of_Sarand%C3%AB "Port of Sarandë"). The rail network is administered by the national railway company [Hekurudha Shqiptare](/wiki/Hekurudha_Shqiptare "Hekurudha Shqiptare"). A new railway line from Tirana and its airport to Durrës is planned. The location of this railway, connecting Albania's most populated urban areas, makes it an important economic development project.[[262]](#cite_note-265)[[263]](#cite_note-266)

### Energy

Main article: [Renewable energy in Albania](/wiki/Renewable_energy_in_Albania "Renewable energy in Albania")

See also: [Power stations](/wiki/List_of_power_stations_in_Albania "List of power stations in Albania") and [Oil fields in Albania](/wiki/List_of_oil_and_gas_fields_in_Albania "List of oil and gas fields in Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Albania_electricity_production.svg/250px-Albania_electricity_production.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Albania_electricity_production.svg)

Electricity production in Albania from 1980 to 2019

Due to its location and natural resources, Albania has a wide variety of [energy resources](/wiki/Energy_resource "Energy resource"), ranging from gas, oil, and coal to [wind](/wiki/Wind_energy "Wind energy"), [solar](/wiki/Solar_energy "Solar energy"), [water](/wiki/Hydropower "Hydropower"), and other [renewable](/wiki/Renewable_energy "Renewable energy") sources.[[264]](#cite_note-267)[[265]](#cite_note-268) According to the [World Economic Forum](/wiki/World_Economic_Forum "World Economic Forum")'s 2023 Energy Transition Index (ETI), the country ranked 21st globally, highlighting the progress in its energy transition agenda.[[266]](#cite_note-269) Currently, Albania's electricity generation sector depends on [hydroelectricity](/wiki/Hydroelectricity "Hydroelectricity"), ranking fifth in the world in percentage terms.[[267]](#cite_note-270)[[268]](#cite_note-271)[[269]](#cite_note-272) Increasing risks of river floods and droughts due to [climate change](/wiki/Climate_change "Climate change") is expected to put electricity generation at risk.[[270]](#cite_note-273) The [Drin](/wiki/Drin_(river) "Drin (river)"), in the north, hosts four [hydroelectric power stations](/wiki/Hydroelectric_power_station "Hydroelectric power station"), including [Fierza](/wiki/Fierza_Hydroelectric_Power_Station "Fierza Hydroelectric Power Station"), [Koman](/wiki/Koman_Hydroelectric_Power_Station "Koman Hydroelectric Power Station"), [Skavica](/wiki/Skavica_Hydro_Power_Plant "Skavica Hydro Power Plant") and [Vau i Dejës](/wiki/Vau_i_Dej%C3%ABs_Hydroelectric_Power_Station "Vau i Dejës Hydroelectric Power Station"). Two other power stations, such as the [Banjë](/wiki/Banj%C3%AB_Hydro_Power_Plant "Banjë Hydro Power Plant") and [Moglicë](/wiki/Moglic%C3%AB_Hydro_Power_Plant "Moglicë Hydro Power Plant"), are along the [Devoll](/wiki/Devoll_(river) "Devoll (river)") in the south.[[271]](#cite_note-274)

Albania has considerable oil deposits. It has the 10th-largest oil reserves in Europe and the 58th in the world.[[272]](#cite_note-275) The country's main petroleum deposits are located around the [Albanian Adriatic Sea Coast](/wiki/Albanian_Adriatic_Sea_Coast "Albanian Adriatic Sea Coast") and [Myzeqe](/wiki/Myzeqe "Myzeqe") Plain within the [Western Lowlands](/wiki/Western_Lowlands "Western Lowlands"), where the country's largest reserve is located. [Patos-Marinza](/wiki/Patos-Marinza_Oil_Field "Patos-Marinza Oil Field"), also located within the area, is the largest onshore [oil field](/wiki/Oil_field "Oil field") in Europe.[[273]](#cite_note-276) The [Trans Adriatic Pipeline](/wiki/Trans_Adriatic_Pipeline "Trans Adriatic Pipeline") (TAP), part of the planned [Southern Gas Corridor](/wiki/Southern_Gas_Corridor "Southern Gas Corridor"), runs for 215 kilometres (134 miles) across Albania's territory before entering the [Albanian Adriatic Sea Coast](/wiki/Albanian_Adriatic_Sea_Coast "Albanian Adriatic Sea Coast") approximately 17 kilometres (11 miles) northwest of [Fier](/wiki/Fier "Fier").[[274]](#cite_note-277)

Albania's [water resources](/wiki/Water_resources "Water resources") are particularly abundant in all the regions of the country and comprise [lakes](/wiki/Lakes_of_Albania "Lakes of Albania"), [rivers](/wiki/Rivers_of_Albania "Rivers of Albania"), springs, and groundwater aquifers.[[275]](#cite_note-278) The country's available average quantity of [fresh water](/wiki/Fresh_water "Fresh water") is estimated at 129.7 cubic metres (4,580 cubic feet) per inhabitant per year, one of the highest rates in Europe.[[276]](#cite_note-279) According to data presented by the [Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply and Sanitation](/wiki/Joint_Monitoring_Programme_for_Water_Supply_and_Sanitation "Joint Monitoring Programme for Water Supply and Sanitation") (JMP) in 2015, about 93% of the country's total population had access to [improved sanitation](/wiki/Improved_sanitation "Improved sanitation").[[277]](#cite_note-280)

### Media

Main article: [Media in Albania](/wiki/Media_in_Albania "Media in Albania")

See also: [Cinema of Albania](/wiki/Cinema_of_Albania "Cinema of Albania")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Villa_of_the_former_Radio_Tirana_%2803%29.jpg/250px-Villa_of_the_former_Radio_Tirana_%2803%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Villa_of_the_former_Radio_Tirana_(03).jpg)

The former grounds of the headquarters of [Radio Tirana](/wiki/Radio_Tirana "Radio Tirana") in the capital of [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana "Tirana"). [Radio Televizioni Shqiptar](/wiki/Radio_Televizioni_Shqiptar "Radio Televizioni Shqiptar") (RTSH) was initially inaugurated as Radio Tirana in 1938 prior to [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").

The [freedom of press](/wiki/Freedom_of_press "Freedom of press") and [speech](/wiki/Freedom_of_speech "Freedom of speech"), and the right to free expression is guaranteed in the [constitution of Albania](/wiki/Constitution_of_Albania "Constitution of Albania").[[278]](#cite_note-281) Albania was ranked 84th on the [Press Freedom Index](/wiki/Press_Freedom_Index "Press Freedom Index") of 2020 compiled by the [Reporters Without Borders](/wiki/Reporters_Without_Borders "Reporters Without Borders"), with its score steadily declining since 2003.[[279]](#cite_note-282) Nevertheless, in the 2020 report of [Freedom in the World](/wiki/Freedom_in_the_World "Freedom in the World"), the [Freedom House](/wiki/Freedom_House "Freedom House") classified the freedoms of press and speech in Albania as partly free from political interference and manipulation.[[280]](#cite_note-283)

[Radio Televizioni Shqiptar](/wiki/Radio_Televizioni_Shqiptar "Radio Televizioni Shqiptar") (RTSH) is the [national broadcaster](/wiki/National_broadcaster "National broadcaster") corporation of Albania operating numerous television and radio stations in the country.[[281]](#cite_note-284) The three major private broadcaster corporations are [Top Channel](/wiki/Top_Channel "Top Channel"), [Televizioni Klan](/wiki/TV_Klan "TV Klan") and [Vizion Plus](/wiki/Vizion_Plus "Vizion Plus") whose content are distributed throughout Albania and beyond its territory in [Kosovo](/wiki/Kosovo "Kosovo") and other [Albanian-speaking](/wiki/Albanian_language "Albanian language") territories.

Albanian cinema has its roots in the 20th century and developed after the country's [declaration of independence](/wiki/Albanian_Declaration_of_Independence "Albanian Declaration of Independence").[[282]](#cite_note-Cinema-285) The first [movie theater](/wiki/Movie_theater "Movie theater") exclusively devoted to showing [motion pictures](/wiki/Motion_pictures "Motion pictures") was built in 1912 in [Shkodër](/wiki/Shkod%C3%ABr "Shkodër").[[282]](#cite_note-Cinema-285) During the [People's Republic of Albania](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_Albania "People's Republic of Albania"), Albanian cinema developed rapidly with the inauguration of the [Kinostudio Shqipëria e Re](/wiki/Kinostudio_Shqip%C3%ABria_e_Re "Kinostudio Shqipëria e Re") in Tirana.[[282]](#cite_note-Cinema-285) In 1953, the Albanian-Soviet [epic film](/wiki/Epic_film "Epic film"), the [Great Warrior Skanderbeg](/wiki/The_Great_Warrior_Skanderbeg "The Great Warrior Skanderbeg"), was released chronicling the life and fight of the medieval Albanian hero [Skanderbeg](/wiki/Gjergj_Kastriot_Skanderbeg "Gjergj Kastriot Skanderbeg"). It went on to win the international prize at the [1954 Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/1954_Cannes_Film_Festival "1954 Cannes Film Festival"). In 2003, the [Tirana International Film Festival](/wiki/Tirana_International_Film_Festival "Tirana International Film Festival") was established, the largest film festival in the country. The [Durrës Amphitheatre](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs_Amphitheatre "Durrës Amphitheatre") is host to the [Durrës International Film Festival](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs_International_Film_Festival "Durrës International Film Festival"), the second largest film festival.

### Technology

Main articles: [Technology](/wiki/Science_and_technology_in_Albania "Science and technology in Albania") and [Telecommunications in Albania](/wiki/Telecommunications_in_Albania "Telecommunications in Albania")

After the [fall of communism](/wiki/Fall_of_communism_in_Albania "Fall of communism in Albania") in 1991, human resources in sciences and technology in Albania have drastically decreased. As of various reports, during 1991 to 2005 approximately 50% of the professors and scientists of the universities and science institutions in the country have left Albania.[[283]](#cite_note-dfid-286) Albania was ranked 67th in the [Global Innovation Index](/wiki/Global_Innovation_Index "Global Innovation Index") in 2025.[[284]](#cite_note-287)[[285]](#cite_note-288)[[286]](#cite_note-289)

Telecommunication represents one of the fastest growing and dynamic sectors in Albania.[[287]](#cite_note-National_Plan-290)[[288]](#cite_note-291) [Vodafone Albania](/wiki/Vodafone_Albania "Vodafone Albania"), [Telekom Albania](/wiki/Telekom_Albania "Telekom Albania") and [Albtelecom](/wiki/Albtelecom "Albtelecom") are the three large providers of [mobile](/wiki/Mobile_network_operator "Mobile network operator") and [internet](/wiki/Internet "Internet") in Albania.[[287]](#cite_note-National_Plan-290) As of the [Electronic and Postal Communications Authority](/wiki/Electronic_and_Postal_Communications_Authority_(Albania) "Electronic and Postal Communications Authority (Albania)") (AKEP) in 2018, the country had approximately 2.7 million active mobile users with almost 1.8 million active broadband subscribers.[[289]](#cite_note-AKEP-292) Vodafone Albania alone served more than 931,000 mobile users, Telekom Albania had about 605,000 users and Albtelecom had more than 272,000 users.[[289]](#cite_note-AKEP-292) In [January 2023](/wiki/List_of_spaceflight_launches_in_January%E2%80%93June_2023 "List of spaceflight launches in January–June 2023"), Albania launched its [first two satellites](/wiki/List_of_first_satellites_by_country "List of first satellites by country"), *Albania 1* and *Albania 2*, into [orbit](/wiki/Orbit "Orbit"), in what was regarded as a milestone effort in monitoring the country's territory and identifying illegal activities.[[290]](#cite_note-293)[[291]](#cite_note-294) Albanian-American engineer [Mira Murati](/wiki/Mira_Murati "Mira Murati"), the Chief Technology Officer of research organisation [OpenAI](/wiki/OpenAI "OpenAI"), played a substantial role in the development and launch of artificial intelligence services such as [ChatGPT](/wiki/ChatGPT "ChatGPT"), [Codex](/wiki/OpenAI_Codex_(AI_agent) "OpenAI Codex (AI agent)") and [DALL-E](/wiki/DALL-E "DALL-E").[[292]](#cite_note-295)[[293]](#cite_note-296)[[294]](#cite_note-297) In December 2023, Prime Minister Edi Rama announced plans for collaboration between the Albanian government and ChatGPT, facilitated by discussions with Murati.[[295]](#cite_note-Taylor-298)[[296]](#cite_note-299) Rama emphasised the intention to streamline the alignment of Albanian laws with the regulations of the European Union, aiming to reduce costs associated with translation and legal services.[[295]](#cite_note-Taylor-298)

## Demographics

Main article: [Demography of Albania](/wiki/Demographics_of_Albania "Demographics of Albania")

See also: [Albanian people](/wiki/Albanian_people "Albanian people") and [Origin of the Albanians](/wiki/Origin_of_the_Albanians "Origin of the Albanians")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/Albania_demography.svg/250px-Albania_demography.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Albania_demography.svg)

Development of the [population of Albania](/wiki/Demographics_of_Albania "Demographics of Albania") over the last sixty years

The demographic statistics of Albania, as revealed by the 2023 census conducted by the [Instituti i Statistikave](/wiki/Institute_of_Statistics_(Albania) "Institute of Statistics (Albania)") (INSTAT), indicated a population of 2,402,113, with a notable decline from the 2,821,977 recorded in the 2011 census.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024105-4)[[297]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2011_Albanian_census20127-300) The decrease in inhabitants began after the disintegration of the communist regime in Albania and is associated with significant shifts within the political, economic, and social structure of Albania.[[298]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPopulation_Dynamics201425-301)[[299]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024114-302) A principal factor in this transition incorporates a decline in fertility rates coupled with an increase in emigration, both contributing to persistent demographic changes and challenges.[[300]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPopulation_Dynamics201439-303) It is forecast that the population will continue shrinking for the next decade at least, depending on the actual rates and the level of migration.[[301]](#cite_note-304) Currently, the population density of Albania is measured at 83.6 inhabitants per square kilometer with a varied distribution of inhabitants across different regions.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024105-4)[[302]](#cite_note-305) The counties of [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana_County "Tirana County") and [Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs_County "Durrës County") showcase substantial concentrations of people, accounting for about 41% of the overall demographic of Albania, with 32% residing in Tirana and 9% in Durrës.[[303]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024117-306) Conversely, more peripheral and rural counties such as [Gjirokastër](/wiki/Gjirokast%C3%ABr_County "Gjirokastër County") and [Kukës](/wiki/Kuk%C3%ABs_County "Kukës County") present significantly lower population densities, with each aiding 3% to the overall population.[[303]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024117-306)

Historically, the Albanian people have established several communities in many regions throughout Southern Europe. The [Albanian diaspora](/wiki/Albanian_diaspora "Albanian diaspora") has been formed since the late Middle Ages, when they emigrated to escape either various socio-political difficulties or the Ottoman conquest of Albania.[[304]](#cite_note-307) Following the fall of communism, large numbers of Albanians have migrated to countries such as Australia, Canada, Switzerland, the United Kingdom and the United States. Albanian [minorities](/wiki/Minority_group "Minority group") are present in the neighbouring territories such as the west of North Macedonia, the southeast of Montenegro, Kosovo in its entirety and parts of southern Serbia. Altogether, the number of ethnic Albanians living abroad is estimated to be higher than the total population inside Albania. As much as a third of those born in the country's borders now live outside of it, making Albania one of the countries with the highest rate of outmigration relative to its population in the world.[[305]](#cite_note-308)[[306]](#cite_note-309) In 2022 the birth rate was 20% lower than in 2021, largely due to emigration of people of childbearing age.[[307]](#cite_note-310)

### Urbanisation

Main article: [List of cities and towns in Albania](/wiki/List_of_cities_and_towns_in_Albania "List of cities and towns in Albania")

Subsequent to the collapse of communism in 1991, Albania has undergone a remarkable transformation in its urban landscape, emerging as one of the fastest urbanising countries in Europe.[[308]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEInternal_Migration_Albania201412-311)[[309]](#cite_note-312)[[310]](#cite_note-313) At the forefront of this transformation is the [Tirana](/wiki/Tirana_County "Tirana County")–[Durrës](/wiki/Durr%C3%ABs_County "Durrës County") agglomeration, a densely populated urban corridor situated along the [western coast of Albania](/wiki/Albanian_Coastal_Lowlands "Albanian Coastal Lowlands").[[311]](#cite_note-CIEACA_2-314) This corridor has become the primary locus of population growth and settlement development, attracting a significant influx of internal migrants from the country's peripheral areas.[[311]](#cite_note-CIEACA_2-314) Despite an overall decline of the country's total population, the proportion of the urban demographic has consistently progressed from 47% in 2001 to 65% in 2023.[[299]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024114-302)[[312]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2011_Albanian_census201210-315)[[313]](#cite_note-316) This sustained increase, coupled with the concentration in the Tirana-Durrës region, has led to a spread of regional imbalances, with the peripheral areas, particularly [Dibër](/wiki/Dib%C3%ABr_County "Dibër County") and [Kukës](/wiki/Kuk%C3%ABs_County "Kukës County"), experiencing severe depopulation.[[314]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEInternal_Migration_Albania201412_&_18-317)[[315]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEInternal_Migration_Albania201415-318)[[303]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTE2023_Albanian_census2024117-306)
